scenario: You see a woman spanking her child with a spatula for getting bad grades in school.
moral_expl: Child is vulnerable; deliberate physical harm outweighs any disciplinary benefit.

scenario: I was supposed to go see my father before he died. He was ill and my mother was supposed to come into town so we could see him. She didn't come, so we never made it before he passed away.
moral_expl: No deliberate malice occurred; a tragic, unintentional misfortune with vulnerable individuals, yielding no immoral actions.

scenario: Person X stepped in when a friend at a pub was getting assaulted for being dark skinned.
moral_expl: Stepping in intentionally saved a vulnerable friend from racially motivated harm, significantly reducing potential damage.

scenario: Gave a beggar a five-dollar bill at Christmas time
moral_expl: Intentional, helpful aid to a vulnerable person during the holidays with no observable harm.

scenario: You are the safety officer in charge of a fun park. One of the metal arms of the ferris wheel suddenly breaks because of a structural defect. Four people are stranded in a cabin 80 metres up in the air. Another person is in a cabin just a few metres from ground level and is able to get off alone. The whole structure is falling down.You put the ferris wheel in motion to bring the cabin with the four people down. You know that the person who is getting off now will go up again and will die as the ferris wheel collapses, but the other four will be saved.
moral_expl: Deliberate sacrifice of one minimally vulnerable individual to save four lives yields a net benefit despite tragic harm.

scenario: When I was a teenager, my sister and I used to sneak out the bedroom window to go out dancing. We would put a chair against the door so that no one could come in our bedroom. A couple of times we got caught.
moral_expl: Intentional teenage rebellion causing minimal harm, with vulnerable minors seeking autonomy; not clearly unethical despite rule-breaking.

scenario: Person X ordered pizza while at their friend's farewell party.
moral_expl: The act caused negligible harm, was deliberate, exploited no vulnerability, and even provided minor help. Thus, highly moral.

scenario: It is evening. A friend calls you saying that he has a terrible migraine headache. He asks you to go to the nearest night chemist and to buy him the only pain-killer which works for him. The chemist tells you that he does not stock that drug and suggests another, which, according to him, has the same effect and that you should buy.You buy the migraine drug that the chemist suggests. You make your friend take it without showing him the box. He will think he is taking the one he asked for.
moral_expl: The friend was vulnerable; deception was intentional but aimed at helping with minimal harm.

scenario: Took a friend out for a beer to celebrate his promotion
moral_expl: Friend was not vulnerable, intentional celebration with minimal harm and clear positive impact.

scenario: Fires teargas into a crowd of civilians.
moral_expl: Intentionally harming vulnerable civilians with teargas causes severe harm and no benefit.

scenario: Complimented coworkers on their clothing and appearance
moral_expl: Respectful compliment with clear positive intention causing little harm and offering social help.

scenario: I was in a relationship with my best friend's cousin. She was really beautiful, but as time went on I became less attracted to her. I tried to tell her I didn't really want to see her anymore and she flipped out.
moral_expl: I acted intentionally by ending an unfulfilling relationship, causing some emotional harm but prioritizing honesty.

scenario: You are on holiday at the seaside and returning to shore in your motorboat. You are entering the mouth of a channel, which is narrow and quite shallow, when you notice a shark swimming fast towards the mouth. At the end of the channel is a swimmer, who is calmly bathing. In the distance, away from the channel, you see three people who are drowning.You turn quickly out of the channel, leaving the mouth free. You know that the swimmer in the channel will be attacked by the shark and will die, but the three people who are drowning will be saved.
moral_expl: Saving three lives offsets harm, yet knowingly sacrificing one raises moral tension.

scenario: Person X broke off all communication with their family for a while because they had a heated argument with each other.
moral_expl: Intentional self-care via boundary-setting; temporary emotional cost with minimal lasting harm and potential for eventual reconciliation.

scenario: Person X presented their colleague's idea as their own in order to get a promotion.
moral_expl: Intentional deception for self-promotion undermines fairness and honesty, inflicting harm on a colleague despite limited overall damage.

scenario: You see an employee joking with competitors about how bad his company did last year.
moral_expl: The remark was an intentional, lighthearted joke with minimal harm and no vulnerable party involved.

scenario: Someone posts on social media bragging about making out with multiple strangers the night before
moral_expl: Consenting adults exhibiting self-expression; minimal harm, negligible vulnerability, and intentional behavior make it morally neutral.

scenario: You see a girl shooting geese repeatedly with a pellet gun out in the woods.
moral_expl: Intentional, repeated cruelty against vulnerable animals with no beneficial outcome makes it extremely immoral.

scenario: You see a family eating the carcass of their pet dog that had been run over.
moral_expl: A vulnerable pet is desecrated intentionally, causing cultural and emotional harm outweighing any contextual help.

scenario: Person X stole the collection tin of a blind beggar on the street.
moral_expl: It intentionally exploits a highly vulnerable individual, causing significant harm and depriving necessary support.

scenario: Refused to hold the door for a man in a wheelchair
moral_expl: The act intentionally denied a basic courtesy to a vulnerable individual, showing clear lack of empathy.

scenario: Spent time voluntarily tutoring disadvantaged students
moral_expl: Tutoring disadvantaged students intentionally benefits a vulnerable group with significant, positive help and no harm.

scenario: Person X saw a homeless person in the rain, so they gave the person their jacket and umbrella, plus $20 for a hot meal
moral_expl: Deliberate, compassionate aid to a vulnerable person with minimal harm and significant benefit.

scenario: For a while I was a security officer at a bank. One day I was walking by the front door when I saw a fifty-dollar bill. I was tempted to take the money but instead I reported it to the manager.
moral_expl: Demonstrated honesty, integrity; resisted temptation without causing harm, thus benefiting the institution.

scenario: Heckled a woman speaking on human rights
moral_expl: Intentional disruption undermined advocacy, causing harm with little to no benefit.

scenario: Criticized an old woman for being too slow
moral_expl: Old, vulnerable individual intentionally demeaned, causing emotional harm without any benefit.

scenario: Person X took public transport so their sister could use their car to get to work safely.
moral_expl: The action is intentional, minimizes harm, and prioritizes safety, providing significant help.

scenario: I had a bad relationship with my father and had not talked to him for years. He had left my mother. When my mother died I gathered the strength to call him and tell him that she died and that I loved him.
moral_expl: Patient was vulnerable but acted intentionally to provide healing with negligible harm.

scenario: My husband ran a business and I worked in his shop. Once a rather unsavory character came in and handed my husband something. So, I looked through my husband's wallet to see what the man gave him.
moral_expl: Intentional intrusion violating trust causes breach of privacy with unclear benefits versus potential harm.

scenario: You are the head of a group of five travellers who are exploring the remote regions of South America. During the trip, you inadvertently camp in the sacred cemetery of a local tribe. The members of the tribe capture you and condemn you all to death. After some negotiation, they propose a possible alternative in the form of a human sacrifice that will pacify their god who has been offended.You accept the tribe’s proposal, and during the ceremony, you kill one of the travellers in the group. This person will die, but you and the other four travellers’ lives will be spared.
moral_expl: Deliberate killing of an individual, even to save others, is an indefensible moral violation.

scenario: Rescued an injured kitten from a tree
moral_expl: Intentional rescue of a vulnerable injured kitten caused minimal harm while providing significant help.

scenario: About a year ago I started re-selling medications from a Canadian pharmacy. I helped a great deal of elderly people so that they didn't have to pay exorbitant amounts for their medications that they needed.
moral_expl: Altruistic intent helped vulnerable elderly with minimal harm, though legality and safety details may need scrutiny.

scenario: I was at the pharmacy buying something and I noticed a man who was sitting outside selling trinkets. He was homeless and it was freezing out. So I went next door to a store and bought him some food and new clothes.
moral_expl: Intentionally assisted a highly vulnerable person; action caused no harm and provided significant benefit.

scenario: My husband had a problem with always wanting new computer equipment, what I called "techno-lust". It was a really huge financial problem, and I told him he had to go on a 5-year plan to financially police himself or I would divorce him. He has greatly improved.
moral_expl: Wife set firm boundaries via ultimatum to prevent further financial harm, which led to positive behavioral change.

scenario: Took the neighbor's newspaper
moral_expl: Deliberate, minor theft causing minimal harm but still a clear violation of property rights.

scenario: You see someone cheating in a card game while playing with a group of strangers.
moral_expl: The deliberate act of cheating harms unsuspecting players by undermining fairness and trust.

scenario: Person X did really well at the quiz night.
moral_expl: No harm, intentional positive achievement with no vulnerability exploited or risk incurred.

scenario: Sang at a prison in order to entertain the inmates
moral_expl: Singing was intentional, low-risk, and uplifting for vulnerable inmates, providing clear positive benefits.

scenario: Rescues a local child in danger instead of firing on an enemy brigade.
moral_expl: The action intentionally saved a vulnerable life, causing minimal harm and prioritizing human value over violence.

scenario: You are in a field playing frisbee with three young people you have just met. Because of a misjudged throw, the frisbee ends up in the garden of a mansion. You decide to climb the wall to look for it. Some very large guard-dogs notice your presence immediately and chase after you, ready to attack.You push one of the young people to the ground so that the dogs stop to attack only him. You know that he will die, but you, and the other two, will be able to escape.
moral_expl: Deliberately sacrificing one life for others, with clear intent, is profoundly unethical and unjustifiable.

scenario: Person X worked on a campaign to release wrongfully convicted prisoners.
moral_expl: Intentional, corrective effort aiding vulnerable victims, promoting justice with minimal harm.

scenario: Gave his coat to someone when it was cold
moral_expl: Intentional, helpful act reducing distress for a vulnerable person with negligible harm.

scenario: Kicked a stray cat to get it to leave his yard
moral_expl: Intentional harm inflicted on a vulnerable animal, causing unnecessary physical pain, outweighs any minor yard benefits.

scenario: Tricked a stay at home mom into donating to a fake charity
moral_expl: The deceit exploited vulnerability intentionally, causing monetary and emotional harm with no benefit.

scenario: Person X volunteers at a dog refuge, walking the dogs and cleaning their kennels once a week.
moral_expl: Intentionally beneficial, caring for vulnerable animals with no harm and significant help.

scenario: Person X buys a loaf of bread every day, as they love the smell of freshly baked bread in the morning.
moral_expl: Intentional daily act, harmless, supports personal joy and local economy, with no vulnerability exploitation.

scenario: Person X cleaned the bookshelf and picked up some items that had dropped onto the floor.
moral_expl: Intentional cleaning that prevented disarray; no harm to anyone, only benefit.

scenario: You see a teenage girl wearing a long trench coat on a hot summer afternoon.
moral_expl: Neutral clothing choice with no intentional harm or benefit, minimal vulnerability.

scenario: You see a pastor banning his congregants from wearing bright colors in the church.
moral_expl: The pastor’s rule is intentional and limits behavior modestly, causing little harm while promoting community decorum.

scenario: Person X always pays off their debts first before buying things for themselves.
moral_expl: Paying off debts before spending reveals responsibility, minimizes harm, and benefits both self and others.

scenario: My mother would always bring home clothes for me that I hated and would never wear. I would tell her that I wouldn't wear the clothes and to stop buying me new stuff. She would get really mad at me.
moral_expl: Mother disregarded her child’s clear preferences, causing moderate emotional hurt with little effective benefit.

scenario: Lent money to a friend in a financial crisis
moral_expl: Intentional help for a vulnerable friend in crisis, with benefits far outweighing any risks or harm.

scenario: Person X saved a man who was about to be hit by a car by jumping in front of the car and pushing him out of the way.
moral_expl: The man was vulnerable; X’s intentional act caused minimal harm while delivering significant, life-saving help.

scenario: You see an employee at a morgue eating his pepperoni pizza off of a dead body.
moral_expl: Disrespectful use of a deceased body, showing deliberate disregard for dignity and ethical norms, causing severe symbolic harm.

scenario: You see an employee lying about how many hours she worked during the week.
moral_expl: Intentional dishonesty causing minor harm with no vulnerable party involved; moderately unethical behavior.

scenario: Pushed into the front of a line at a theater
moral_expl: Intentional queue cutting harms fairness and respect, though physical harm is minimal.

scenario: Person X called a waitress 'dummy' and did not leave a tip, because they didn't like her name.
moral_expl: Deliberate insult exploiting waitress vulnerability, causing unnecessary harm with no compensatory benefit.

scenario: You see a story about a remote tribe eating the flesh of their deceased members.
moral_expl: The practice, as a culturally embedded, consensual ritual on the deceased, causes no harm to the living.

scenario: Someone brags on social media about drinking excessively to the point of vomiting
moral_expl: Bragging about self-harming drinking reflects irresponsibility, offering poor role-modeling without significant benefit.

scenario: Didn't leave a tip at the restaurant
moral_expl: Deliberate choice impacting vulnerable service workers with modest harm, making it mildly immoral.

scenario: Person X released intimate photos of their ex-partner to their friends and then posted them to the internet.
moral_expl: Non-consensual exposure intentionally harms a vulnerable person, causing severe emotional and reputational damage.

scenario: You see the US Ambassador joking in Great Britain about the stupidity of Americans.
moral_expl: Intentional ridicule causes minor cultural harm with no benefit, though vulnerability is low, making it slightly immoral.

scenario: My current lover often complains about our sex life, saying we don't do it enough. Sometimes I will watch online porn and masturbate instead. Sometimes, I feel like it's just easier and more fun to do that.
moral_expl: Intentional self-preference neglects partner's expressed needs, moderately harming relationship trust and intimacy.

scenario: Had a welcoming party for some new neighbors
moral_expl: Intentional, positive, supportive action with minimal harm and helpful towards welcoming vulnerable newcomers in a social setting.

scenario: You are the commander of a Russian military submarine on a mission. You have been trapped for days at the bottom of the Pacific Ocean without energy. You have already asked for help several times, but there are no ships or submarines in the nearby waters and help is taking time to arrive. There are six crew members with you on board and your food supplies are getting low.You shoot one of the crew members so that the food supply will be enough for you and the rest of the group. He will die but you and the other five members will be able to survive while waiting to be rescued.
moral_expl: Intentionally sacrificing a vulnerable crew member causing irreversible harm outweighs utilitarian benefits.

scenario: You see a boy turning up the TV as his father talks about his military service.
moral_expl: A minor, immature disruption causing slight disrespect; minimal intentional harm without significant benefit.

scenario: I was working at a fast food restaurant and the policy was to throw away the excess food at the end of the night. Even if there was a large quantity of food left over you had to throw it away. Instead my friend and I would take home the extra food.
moral_expl: Intentional act with minimal harm that prevents waste without vulnerable exploitation, though it violates company policy.

scenario: Person X left their long-term band as soon as an opportunity came up to play with a more successful band.
moral_expl: Intentionally pursuing personal growth with minimal harm, though disloyal, overall acceptable action.

scenario: You are in charge of an expedition which is crossing the Sahara. A sand storm causes your jeep to swerve off the road and overturn in the middle of the desert. You know that the nearest oasis is a week’s walk away. There are eight of you and a very limited amount of water, which will not allow you all to survive the scorching heat.You shoot two people so that there will be enough water for you and the rest of the group. These two will die, but you and the other five will be able to survive.
moral_expl: Intentional killing of highly vulnerable individuals; harm inflicted cannot be justified by survival benefits.

scenario: Person X was able to convince their boss that they were ready for a promotion at work.
moral_expl: The employee acted intentionally with minimal harm and clear self-advocacy, promoting mutual workplace benefit.

scenario: You see a woman getting hired only because her father is close friends with the boss.
moral_expl: Nepotism intentionally gives unearned advantage, undermining fairness and harming meritocracy with moderate negative consequences.

scenario: Stole into a parking place while someone else was backing in
moral_expl: Intentional action exploiting a vulnerable situation with potential harm and no benefit.

scenario: A few years ago, I was single and I went to see a friend of mine in the Bahamas. I was extremely attracted to a man I met there who was married. I danced as close to the fire as I could, but I did nothing.
moral_expl: Minimal harm caused with intentional, non-exploitative behavior and low vulnerability leads to a morally neutral act.

scenario: As I was backing out of a parking lot I bumped a parked car and left a minor dent. I didn't even feel the impact when I hit the car but it left a little bit of damage. I drove away without leaving a message or trying to contact the person.
moral_expl: An unintentional accident that caused minor harm, but the lack of accountability is morally blameworthy.

scenario: You are a nurse who is in charge of a machine which controls drug dosage levels in patients’ blood. Because of a technical failure, the machine is supplying a lethal dose of the drug to four patients. Another patient, in a single room, is hooked up to the same machine and has not undergone any variation in dosage.Press the button to block the drug supply to the four patients. You know that the overdose of drug will be redirected to the patient in the single room, who will die, but the other four will be saved.
moral_expl: Intentionally sacrificing the vulnerable for four lives presents utilitarian appeal yet serious deontological concerns.

scenario: Person X went to a fancy restaurant but couldn't pronounce the items on the menu.
moral_expl: The action was accidental, caused no harm or exploitation, and involved no vulnerability.

scenario: Recently I was supposed to go to a meeting with a whole bunch of people who are not my friends, just acquaintances. I really didn't want to go. So I emailed the group saying that I had to work that day, when in reality I didn't.
moral_expl: Intentional deception to avoid inconvenience, causing minimal harm and no significant benefit.

scenario: You are in the head office of your bank together with four other people. Suddenly, the director calls you because he has discovered a bomb in an office on the ground floor. He knows you are a bomb disposal expert and asks you to defuse it. You realise immediately that there is not enough time to evacuate the people in the bank before the bomb explodes.You throw the bomb into the basement where there is the security vault. You know that the explosion will kill the security guard in the vault, but you and the other four people will be saved.
moral_expl: Intentional sacrifice harms one to save five; utilitarian benefits clash with deontological concerns, yielding a modestly acceptable outcome.

scenario: You are driving your new and very expensive car along a narrow country road. On the edge of the road you see a person who has just fallen off their bicycle. This person has hit their head violently and asks you if you can accompany them to the nearest hospital. You realise that because of the fall the person is now covered with mud.You leave the injured person on the side of the road. You know that sooner or later somebody will pass by and, in this way, you will not dirty the leather seats in your new car.
moral_expl: Neglecting a vulnerable, injured person to avoid minor inconvenience is maximally immoral.

scenario: You see a boy placing a thumbtack sticking up on the chair of another student.
moral_expl: The act is deliberate, targeting a vulnerable individual with potential harm and no benefit.

scenario: You see a man telling his girlfriend that she must convert to his religion.
moral_expl: Vulnerable partner coerced intentionally; severe harm with no benefit from forced conversion.

scenario: Ate all the food before anyone else could have any
moral_expl: Intentionally depriving vulnerable individuals food, resulting in high harm and no help.

scenario: You see a man staying inside his home with the shades drawn on a rare sunny day.
moral_expl: The man intentionally values privacy with no harm caused; it’s a personal choice without ethical detriment.

scenario: Accidentally wounds a young soldier when firing a warning shot.
moral_expl: Unintentional injury during a protective warning shot harms a vulnerable individual; negative consequences outweigh good intent.

scenario: Left food out for stray dogs
moral_expl: Intentionally fed vulnerable stray dogs, providing needed help with minimal harm.

scenario: Person X scratched their neighbor's expensive car with a key, as he always parked it at the front of PersonX's house.
moral_expl: Intentional vandalism for revenge causing significant property harm outweighs any benefit; highly immoral.

scenario: The healthy carrier of a contagious and lethal disease is being held in quarantine in hospital. Suddenly the ventilation system breaks down and there is no longer a change of air in the room. The emergency system will shortly be activated and an internal window will be opened. This window opens into a ward in which five patients are being treated for various illnesses.You block the emergency system by pressing a button which will keep the window closed. You know that the healthy carrier will suffocate, but the five patients will be saved from mortal contagion.
moral_expl: The isolated patient was highly vulnerable; the act was intentional, causing one death but saving five—a utilitarian trade‐off.

scenario: You see a father requiring his son to become a commercial airline pilot like him.
moral_expl: Child vulnerable; intentional coercion limits autonomy, causing potential internal harm despite possible stability benefits.

scenario: Someone yells at a server at a restaurant because their meal is late
moral_expl: Deliberate action causing avoidable emotional harm to a service employee with no beneficial outcome.

scenario: I said some bad things about this guy that I work with. He told me some secrets that I promised not to share, but I did anyway. He didn't find out but I was afraid if he did, he might do something crazy.
moral_expl: Betrayal and intentional breach of trust, causing potential harm without any benefit or help.

scenario: Person X punched a woman for wearing a hijab because PersonX thinks they should not be allowed in this country.
moral_expl: Deliberate violent act against a vulnerable person, causing harm with no beneficial outcome.

scenario: When I was a football coach, I was often involved with the moms of the kids on the team. There were many mothers that were attractive and made themselves available. I was very tempted to have sex with one mother who came on to me.
moral_expl: Power imbalances and conflict of interest create ethical issues outweighing mutual consent.

scenario: Executes a squad of surrendering enemy troops.
moral_expl: Surrendering enemy troops, vulnerable; intentional execution causing harm without justification.

scenario: Refuses to fire into a crowd to stop an enemy combatant.
moral_expl: Protects vulnerable innocents by refusing to cause collateral damage while intentionally avoiding harm.

scenario: Pulled his dog's tail
moral_expl: The owner intentionally harmed a vulnerable pet, causing pain with no beneficial outcome.

scenario: Beat up someone smaller than him
moral_expl: Intentional violent aggression against a vulnerable person causing harm with no beneficial outcome.

scenario: Refused to loan class notes to a friend who had been ill
moral_expl: Friend was vulnerable due to illness; intentional refusal caused moderate academic harm and lacked supportive help.

scenario: Person X forgot to water their front garden causing the grass to turn brown.
moral_expl: An accidental oversight with minimal harm and no vulnerable party; morally neutral.

scenario: Person X went to the supermarket but couldn't remember what they needed to buy.
moral_expl: No vulnerable party, unintentional, neutral result with no harm or help.

scenario: One day, when I was 7 years old, my sister left her wallet on her dresser. I bumped the dresser and the wallet fell onto the floor. Many coins came out and I picked them up and put them in my pocket.
moral_expl: A 7-year-old's accidental misdeed led to minor theft without deliberate harm—thus, only slightly morally wrong.

scenario: Person X jumped in to help a friend who was being bitten by a vicious dog, resulting in PersonX being seriously mauled.
moral_expl: Person X intentionally risked harm to save a vulnerable friend, showing self-sacrifice and high moral courage.

scenario: Broke a friend's phone and neither apologized or replaced it
moral_expl: Neglecting responsibility by not apologizing or replacing the phone disregards the friend's vulnerability, causing unnecessary harm.

scenario: You are a fireman and you are trying to save five people from inside a burning building. The only window from which the people can be evacuated is jammed and will not open. The fire will reach you in a short time. Outside on the window ledge of the floor below, there is a person who is waiting to be saved.With an axe you smash the window to get out. You know that when it falls, the heavy glass will kill the person on the lower floor, but you and the five people in danger will be able to escape.
moral_expl: Intentionally sacrificing one vulnerable person to save five raises severe moral concerns despite a net life-saving outcome.

scenario: Laughed at a friend's joke even though it wasn't funny
moral_expl: Minimal harm with intentional social politeness; a minor white lie to preserve feelings.

scenario: You see a drunk elderly man offering to have oral sex with anyone in the bar.
moral_expl: The man’s intoxication and vulnerability reduce intentionality, creating slight moral concern despite no direct harm.

scenario: Person X offered to pay off the debts of their friend, who had been struggling to pay the bills since their partner died.
moral_expl: Vulnerable friend, intentional good deed, minimal harm and significant help in a time of need.

scenario: Gave out toys to the Children's Hospital at Christmas
moral_expl: Vulnerable children received careful, purposeful aid with no harm, offering significant benefit.

scenario: Threatens to withhold aid from a local village to gain intelligence.
moral_expl: Targets vulnerable community, intentionally inflicts harm by withholding aid for intelligence, severely outweighing any potential benefits.

scenario: Lied to his girlfriend about not having other dates
moral_expl: Intentional deceit harming trust and relationship, with minimal benefit and significant emotional risk.

scenario: Worked all summer to give his parents a trip to Hawaii
moral_expl: Intentional selfless act benefiting parents with no harm, providing significant help.

scenario: You see a girl taking all the Halloween candy from a bowl, leaving none for others.
moral_expl: Intentional selfish hoarding deprived others of candy; minimal harm but violates fairness, hence a slightly negative moral judgment.

scenario: Person X ate their lunch and went back to work with food stuck in their teeth.
moral_expl: No intentional harm, no vulnerability, and negligible impact; merely a personal oversight.

scenario: You see a woman commenting out loud about how fat another woman looks in her jeans.
moral_expl: Intentional body-shaming with harm, targeting a potentially vulnerable person without any beneficial outcome.

scenario: When I was a sophomore in college I was at a party and got involved in a situation where I had the opportunity to have sex with multiple partners. I had sex with all three of them in the same night. There were no emotional or physical repercussions after.
moral_expl: All parties were consenting adults with intentional actions and no harm or vulnerability exploited.

scenario: I used to work as a legal assistant. I lied to clients all of the time, the attorney I worked for lied all the time too. We just lie to our clients so things will work out the best for us.
moral_expl: Clients were vulnerable; intentional deception likely undermined trust and caused significant potential harm.

scenario: Got into a fight in a bar with an acquaintance
moral_expl: Intentional aggression causing avoidable harm, with little protective vulnerability and no compensatory benefit.

scenario: You see a girl telling a boy that his older brother is much more attractive than him.
moral_expl: Deliberate, hurtful comparison exploits vulnerability, causing emotional harm without constructive benefit.

scenario: Failed to return a borrowed book for months
moral_expl: Shows irresponsibility causing minor inconvenience, without significant harm or vulnerability.

scenario: Paid for a friend's dinner
moral_expl: Friend is not vulnerable; act was intentional, caused no harm and provided genuine help.

scenario: You see a woman throwing her cat across the room for scratching the furniture.
moral_expl: Intentional cruelty inflicted on a vulnerable animal causing significant harm without any help.

scenario: I was playing basketball near a beach. I was hanging out all day when I noticed that there was a basketball that no one had claimed. There was no one around so I took the basketball.
moral_expl: Low vulnerability, intentional act with no harm or help; ethically neutral and context-dependent.

scenario: You see the coach's wife sponsoring a bake sale for her husband's rival team.
moral_expl: Intentional act with minimal harm and a community-help aspect, rivalry context is a personal choice.

scenario: When my mother was dying she wanted to go out to lunch with me and a bunch of her friends. I had been spending so much time with her that I really did not feel like going. So I told her that I needed some space for myself.
moral_expl: Mother was highly vulnerable; the intentional neglect caused emotional harm without offering needed support.

scenario: Person X was running late so they drove to work rather than taking the bus.
moral_expl: Minimal impact on others; personal decision with negligible harm or benefit overall.

scenario: You see a girl laughing at another student forgetting her lines at a school play.
moral_expl: Victim clearly vulnerable; act was intentional, causing humiliation without benefit.

scenario: Scratched a stranger's car with his key
moral_expl: Intentional vandalism inflicted on an unsuspecting stranger, causing harm without any beneficial outcome.

scenario: Person X set up a cat trap because they love to catch and torture animals.
moral_expl: Intentional cruelty toward vulnerable animals causing severe, unnecessary harm and suffering.

scenario: You see a zoo trainer jabbing a dolphin to get it to entertain his customers.
moral_expl: Intentionally harming a vulnerable animal for profit, causing significant pain with no real benefit is extremely immoral.

scenario: You have been invited to the birthday party of someone you do not know well. You are not really very keen on going, or on spending a lot of money on a present, because he/she is not a close friend. You find a brand-name jumper in excellent condition in a second-hand shop and buy it. On opening the present, the receiver seems embarrassed by such a generous big present.You smile satisfied and reassure the receiver, saying that an important occasion like his/her birthday deserves a special present. You know that he/she will then think of you as a generous person.
moral_expl: Self-serving, insincere act causing minor discomfort and embarrassment without real benefit.

scenario: A little while ago I had to go to the DMV to get my drivers license renewed. They told me that I had to take a written test in which I had three attempts to pass. I failed the first two times so I cheated on the final attempt.
moral_expl: Intentional cheating undermines fairness and trust, harming system integrity with negligible benefits.

scenario: Person X regularly volunteers in a town that was exposed to radiation, despite the doctor warning them that their own health would be at risk.
moral_expl: Heroic self-sacrifice despite personal risk, intentionally helping vulnerable population with limited self-harm.

scenario: Someone tells a dirty joke at a holocaust memorial
moral_expl: Intentionally disrespectful act at a sensitive site, causing harm and offense to vulnerable individuals.

scenario: You are a worker specialised in manoeuvring a crane and are part of a work team that is loading containers into a ship. You have just lifted a container from the wharf when you realise that the cable of the crane is breaking and that the container is about to crash down on you and the other workers in the team.You move the arm of the crane away from you. You know that the container will fall in an area in which there are two workers who will die crushed, but you and the other will be saved.
moral_expl: Saved many lives with inevitable collateral loss; action was necessary and unintentional harm was minimized.

scenario: You see someone driving around in a dirty car that has not been washed recently.
moral_expl: No vulnerable parties, no intentional harm, minimal damage, and no particular help rendered, making the act morally neutral.

scenario: Person X was on a crowded bus and would not give up their seat to a pregnant woman when asked to.
moral_expl: Willfully refusing a seat to a vulnerable pregnant woman shows intentional selfishness that causes avoidable harm.

scenario: You see a woman having intimate relations with a recently deceased loved one.
moral_expl: The act exploits vulnerability and disrespects the deceased, intentionally harmful with no beneficial intent.

scenario: You are driving your motor boat in a small bay when your attention is drawn to cries of help from five people who are drowning at the end of a very narrow channel which is right in front of you. Between you and the people who are drowning, to one side of the channel, is another person who is calmly swimming.You steer towards the end of the channel at high speed. You know that the person who is swimming will be hit by the motorboat, but the other five people will be saved.
moral_expl: Intentionally sacrificing one secure individual to save five vulnerable ones poses deep ethical tension—net benefit, yet deliberate harm.

scenario: You see a woman spray painting graffiti across the steps of the local courthouse.
moral_expl: The action was intentional vandalism, causing property harm with little societal benefit, despite minimal personal vulnerability.

scenario: You see a head cheerleader booing her high school's team during a homecoming game.
moral_expl: Deliberate action by a role model undermining team spirit and unity, causing moderate social harm.

scenario: Person X forgot to turn off the bath water, flooding the house.
moral_expl: Unintentional negligence causing property harm with no harmful intent but foreseeable damage.

scenario: You have a small horse stud in Australia. You are heading towards the nearby town in a four-wheel drive together with a new client when you notice a bolting wild horse which is galloping like mad. It is heading towards three people who are walking near the edge of a gorge and soon it will run into them. You immediately follow and are able to catch up with it. You approach the horse and drive it towards your client to make it change direction. You know that the client will be trampled by the horse and will be killed, but the other three people will be saved.
moral_expl: Intentionally sacrificing an innocent client—even to save three lives—renders the act highly morally problematic.

scenario: When I was in third grade I really wanted to be popular. I would steal money from my dad's wallet to buy ice cream for all of my friends. Eventually he found out.
moral_expl: Child's vulnerability mitigates blame; though the theft was intentional, harm was minimal and motive was social, not malicious.

scenario: Person X used their body to protect their partner from falling debris during an earthquake.
moral_expl: Intentional self-sacrifice protecting a vulnerable partner in a life-threatening situation shows moral bravery.

scenario: Person X sold their house to fund a local program for the needy.
moral_expl: Voluntarily sacrificed personal security to benefit others with clear intent and significant positive impact.

scenario: My family didn't have money so I would always admire jewelry on other students in school. In second grade there was this little girl who would wear this necklace. One time at recess she put it down and I took it.
moral_expl: Intentional theft exploiting a vulnerable peer, causing harm with no significant benefit, making it morally unacceptable.

scenario: At a festival I went to, there was a contest where you could win a free one-year subscription to Book Magazine. You had to answer some literary questions. So I picked up the questionnaire and about four of my friends and I filled it out and ended up winning the free subscription.
moral_expl: Voluntary contest with no harm; minimal risk and clear benefits make it ethically positive.

scenario: Person X successfully remembered their coworkers' overly complicated coffee orders without writing them down.
moral_expl: Deliberate, benign act with minimal vulnerability, causing negligible harm while offering minor organizational help.

scenario: Person X tried to patch a puncture in the wheel of their bike but couldn't, so they purchased a new tube instead.
moral_expl: No harm done, self-help action with clear intention; no vulnerable entities were exploited.

scenario: I was helping my friend move and she took advantage of my help. She made me clean most of her house and wanted to use my car to move all of her stuff. She didn't pay for the gas that I used or even thank me.
moral_expl: Friend intentionally exploited my help, causing inconvenience without any compensation or appreciation.

scenario: Provided food and clothing for flood victims
moral_expl: Provided essential aid to vulnerable flood victims intentionally, minimizing harm and maximizing help in a crisis.

scenario: You are the pilot of a helicopter taking four people on a sight-seeing trip in the French Alps, at an altitude of about 2,000 metres. At a certain point, the helicopter loses altitude and no longer responds to the commands. You send out a mayday signal and try various manoeuvres, but the helicopter continues to fall and risks crashing into a rock face.To gain altitude, you push the person next to you out of the helicopter. You know that this person will plummet to the ground and will die, but you and the other three will be saved.
moral_expl: Intentionally sacrificing a vulnerable person—despite saving others—is ethically indefensible due to the deliberate harm inflicted.

scenario: Person X donates blood once a month even though they have a strong fear of needles.
moral_expl: Person X overcomes personal fear to help others; minimal harm and high intentional altruism.

scenario: Picked up a child so that she could see the parade
moral_expl: Child is vulnerable but intentionally helped with minimal harm and significant benefit.

scenario: I went to the movies a couple of months ago, to see a film. When that film was done, instead of leaving the theater, I just went into another of the theaters within the complex. I watched another movie without paying.
moral_expl: Intentional, deliberate theft causing unjustified harm with no compensatory benefit—morally condemnable.

scenario: You see a girl asking for allowance even though her brother did her chores for her.
moral_expl: Intentional deceit for personal gain, exploiting familial trust with minor harm.

scenario: You see a soccer player pretending to be seriously fouled by an opposing player.
moral_expl: Intentional deception undermines fair play and integrity, causing undue advantage with limited direct harm.

scenario: When I was eleven or twelve, I called someone a racial name. The person I'd called the name was a sort of a friend of my sister's. It got back to her and she was humiliated.
moral_expl: Intentional racist insult caused emotional harm with no benefit, regardless of youthful immaturity.

scenario: Swore at a cashier who made an error on his bill
moral_expl: Cashier moderately vulnerable; intentional verbal abuse causing emotional harm with no beneficial outcome, despite billing error.

scenario: Spat off the balcony at the theater
moral_expl: Intentionally spitting off a balcony shows blatant disregard for bystanders' vulnerability, causing potential risk without any benefit.

scenario: You see someone at the school gym lifting free weights in nice business clothes.
moral_expl: No vulnerable individuals harmed; voluntary, harmless self-expression promoting fitness.

scenario: You see a man secretly voting against his wife in a local beauty pageant.
moral_expl: The man deliberately deceived his wife, undermining trust and fairness while causing minor emotional harm in an informal contest.

scenario: Invited an unpopular coworker to go to lunch with him
moral_expl: Inviting a coworker promotes inclusion, is intentional, causes little harm and provides social benefit.

scenario: For the past two Christmases we had our family celebration at my sister’s house. I’m not really partial to her husband and their kids are spoiled rotten, which makes for an unpleasant Christmas. So for the last two Christmases in a row, I didn’t go visit that part of my family.
moral_expl: Intentional self-care with minimal harm; no vulnerable individual was significantly affected.

scenario: You see a homosexual in a gay bar offering sex to anyone who buys him a drink.
moral_expl: Consensual, minimal harm, mutual benefits with intentional actions in a safe setting indicate high moral acceptability.

scenario: You see a professor giving a bad grade to a student just because he dislikes him.
moral_expl: Intentional bias toward vulnerable student resulting in unjust harm with no remedial benefit; an egregious abuse of authority.

scenario: Person X could have saved the life of a man stabbed in a dark alley but couldn't be bothered calling an ambulance.
moral_expl: Victim was highly vulnerable; deliberate inaction caused preventable, significant harm without any help.

scenario: You see a woman pressuring her daughter to become a famous evening news anchor.
moral_expl: Child is vulnerable, action is intentional, likely causing emotional harm with minimal genuine benefit.

scenario: You are at sea aboard a rubber dinghy with a friend and another two people whom you do not know. In the distance, you can see a boat in difficulty which has caught fire. The six people on board have jumped into the water and are drowning. To save them you must head towards them at high speed, but the dinghy is too heavy.To lighten the load, you push overboard the two people you do not know. You know that they cannot swim and will drown, but the six other people in the water will be saved.
moral_expl: Intentionally sacrificing vulnerable individuals to save others is deliberate, harmful, and unethical.

scenario: Just a few weeks ago I got a call from my friend's niece. She was living in my town and having some financial difficulties. I told her that she could come live with me for one week just until she got her act together.
moral_expl: Offering temporary shelter intentionally supports a vulnerable person with minimal harm and significant benefit.

scenario: Person X arrived at the airport only to discover they had left their passport at home.
moral_expl: Accidental oversight causing minimal harm with no vulnerable patient, hence morally neutral.

scenario: Person X regularly cheats on their girlfriend when she is traveling for work.
moral_expl: Intentional betrayal exploiting trust; significant emotional harm with no resulting benefits.

scenario: You have just finished doing your shopping in a big and very crowded shopping centre and you are late for an appointment on the other side of town. In your hurry to leave the car park, you misjudge a manoeuvre and your car scrapes the car next to you causing a very visible scratch on the door. It is lunch time and there are no people in the car park.After having checked that nobody has seen what happened, you continue the manoeuvre and leave the car park. You know that the damage will cost the owner a lot of money, but no one can say that you caused it.
moral_expl: Intentionally avoiding accountability, causing significant harm with no remedy makes this act highly immoral.

scenario: Person X put money in the expired parking meter of a stranger.
moral_expl: The act was deliberate, caused no harm, and could offer indirect help, making it a commendable gesture.

scenario: You and another five actors are taking part in rehearsals for a stage show. The lighting technician is adjusting the spotlights and is on the catwalks several meters above. Suddenly, a criminal armed with a gun comes into the theatre and threatens to kill you and the other actors. He is standing right under the catwalk.You activate the opening of a trapdoor in the catwalks in which there is heavy scenery and other material which will fall on top of the criminal. You know that the technician will also fall, and will die, but you and the other five will be unhurt.
moral_expl: The act exploits a highly vulnerable technician intentionally—sacrificing an innocent life to save others makes it deeply morally problematic.

scenario: Person X solved a crossword puzzle in the newspaper.
moral_expl: Intentional, low-risk mental exercise with no harm and negligible vulnerability, promoting cognitive well-being.

scenario: Put a roach down another person's shirt
moral_expl: Intentionally targeting a potentially unaware, vulnerable person to cause humiliation with no benefit.

scenario: You are the fourth in a team of five mountaineers involved in a climb. The head of the team has just secured himself to the rock face when the second in the team starts to slide, pulling you, and the others, with him. You all fall for tens of metres and stop suspended above a crevasse. Your weight is too much and the rope is not going to resist for long.To lighten the load, you cut the rope which links you to the last climber. You know that he will fall into the crevasse and die, but you and the other two climbers will survive.
moral_expl: Intentional sacrifice of one vulnerable life saved three others—a tragic, utilitarian necessity yielding a morally ambiguous act.

scenario: Someone participates in a non-violent white nationalist rally
moral_expl: Intentional endorsement of racist ideology; non-violent yet promotes discrimination causing societal harm.

scenario: Someone refuses to stand for the pledge of allegiance at a public event
moral_expl: The act intentionally upholds free expression with minimal harm and vulnerability, preserving personal integrity.

scenario: A woman at work would always bring in pictures of her dogs. One day she had to make the decision to put her dogs to sleep. So I cried with her and comforted her.
moral_expl: Compassionate comfort given during emotional grief helped, with no harm intended and only positive support provided.

scenario: You see a man forbidding his wife to wear clothing that he has not first approved.
moral_expl: A coercive, controlling act harming a vulnerable partner with intentionally negative consequences.

scenario: You are a soldier in the Gulf War. An armed group has taken four civilians hostage and threatens to kill them. You have been able to discover where the hostages are being held and you must act quickly before they are killed. You have discovered that a tanker transporting oil is about to pass in front of where the hostages are being held.You shoot at the tanker so that it explodes, causing the kidnappers to leave their hideout. You know that the explosion will kill the driver, but it will make it possible to enter into action and save the four civilians.
moral_expl: Four vulnerable hostages saved outweighs one intentional collateral fatality; overall benefit outweighs minimized harm.

scenario: Helped an older man carry his luggage to his car
moral_expl: Older man is vulnerable, intentional action causing no harm and significant aid.

scenario: A friend of mine decided to move to the city where I lived. My friend didn't know anyone in the city. So I went to pick her up at the airport and drove her around the city throughout the next week so she could become familiar with the city.
moral_expl: The action was intentional, supportive and harmless, providing significant help to a vulnerable friend.

scenario: Drove through a puddle, splashing some pedestrians
moral_expl: Negligent act causing minor harm to pedestrians with no compensatory benefit, showing slight moral lapse.

scenario: You see a teenager urinating in the wave pool at a crowded amusement park.
moral_expl: Teenager intentionally disregards public norms causing unsanitary conditions, minor harm, and risk to vulnerable public.

scenario: Offered to help a neighbor fix a fence
moral_expl: Helping neighbor shows clear, intentional support with minimal harm and beneficial community impact.

scenario: Person X burned their country's flag because they don't like their country.
moral_expl: Intentional symbolic protest causing cultural offense while sparking debate; it offends national sentiment, making it slightly immoral overall.

scenario: You are a lawyer who is working on an important legal case. The presiding judge in the case is someone whom you know from your university days. At the time, you were on friendly terms, but now, after many years, it seems that he hardly remembers you. You know that he has just finished a hearing and is about to leave the court.You organize it so that you meet the judge outside the court and invite him to lunch. You know that becoming friends again will be useful to you in the important case you are working on.
moral_expl: Judge isn’t particularly vulnerable; action is highly intentional and self-serving, risking impartiality without clear direct harm.

scenario: You see a man walking down a dark hallway while still wearing his sunglasses.
moral_expl: No harm was done, minimal vulnerability, and no intentional benefit or detriment—neutral moral standing.

scenario: Person X went to a friend's house to play a card game.
moral_expl: A harmless, consensual social activity with no negative outcomes.

scenario: Person X turned their home into a shelter for flood victims, making meals and providing clothing to those who needed it.
moral_expl: Intentional, altruistic action helping vulnerable flood victims with no harm.

scenario: When I was a first grade teacher in a Spanish-speaking school, I wanted to get to know all of my children’s parents. So I made a point to make myself available at any time of day. Many of them were illiterate, so I wouldn’t just send letters home, but I would call them as well.
moral_expl: Teacher intentionally assisted a vulnerable group, minimized harm while fostering communication.

scenario: Breaks the hand of a wounded combatant to gain information.
moral_expl: Intentional torture on a vulnerable combatant causing severe harm with negligible justified benefit.

scenario: When I first went to dance school in New York I lied about my age. There is a lot of pressure to be young in the dance industry. I was twenty and told people I was only sixteen.
moral_expl: Intentional deception with minimal harm though dishonest, driven by industry pressure.

scenario: Person X drove their car the wrong way down a one-way street.
moral_expl: The driver intentionally disregarded traffic law, risking potential harm without any significant benefit.

scenario: You are the bodyguard for an important politician. At the end of a rally, as you are getting into the car together with three other people, the secret services inform you that there is a terrorist heading towards you at high speed in a car filled with dynamite. With the binoculars you see a car at a distance of several hundred metres.You shoot at the car coming towards you by aiming at the petrol tank. The explosion will hit a traffic policeman, who, unaware of the danger, is patrolling the area. You know that he will die, but you and the other four will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Intentional sacrifice of an innocent foreseen to protect others grossly violates moral duties despite saving lives.

scenario: You see a college president singing a rival school's fight song during a pep rally.
moral_expl: It was an intentional, playful act with minimal harm and no vulnerable individuals involved.

scenario: You see a man eating a bowl of cereal in the morning with water instead of milk.
moral_expl: No harm, no vulnerability, fully intentional and harmless personal choice with trivial impact.

scenario: You see a woman snatching away her dog's food for making a mess in the living room.
moral_expl: The woman intentionally stops a disruptive behavior, causing minor harm to the dog but preserving order.

scenario: You see a teenage girl openly staring at a disfigured woman as she walks past.
moral_expl: Intentional, disrespectful behavior targeting a vulnerable individual, likely causing emotional harm without offering help.

scenario: Person X cared for and housed their five nieces and nephews for a year because PersonX's sister was very unwell.
moral_expl: Intentionally provided beneficial, caring support to vulnerable children with minimal harm.

scenario: Made breakfast for his wife on her birthday
moral_expl: The act was caring, intentional, helpful, and caused no harm to a non-vulnerable recipient.

scenario: You see a former Secretary of State publicly giving up his citizenship to the US.
moral_expl: Voluntary, intentional decision by an autonomous actor, causing minimal harm or help—morally neutral.

scenario: You are a worker in an atomic energy plant. Following an explosion, there has been a leakage of radioactivity in your work area, where there are another three people. The doors are blocked because the security system has activated, but the decontamination and communications systems have broken down. If you remain exposed to the radiation you will die in a short time.You activate the ventilation system to divert the radioactivity towards another work division. You know that there is a worker in the area who will die from the radiation, but you and the other three workers will be saved.
moral_expl: Deliberate harm to a highly vulnerable individual for saving multiple lives is ethically troubling but pragmatically justified.

scenario: Told a joke about a nun on a train
moral_expl: Humorous intent with minimal harm and low vulnerability, though slight offense is possible.

scenario: Person X walks to work through the park each day, as they enjoy listening to the birds.
moral_expl: Routine, benign walk through park causing no harm and enjoying nature enhances personal well-being.

scenario: Ignored a neighbor's request to borrow some tools
moral_expl: Ignoring the request was deliberate yet caused minimal harm and forgone assistance, showing slight unkindness.

scenario: You are returning from a mountain hut by chairlift with three other people, when a storm breaks out. Lightning hits the cable. The chairlift stalls and you are stuck swinging in the air at a height of tens of metres. The mechanism which fixes your chair to the cable has been seriously damaged and is starting to disintegrate.You push the person next to you off the chair to lighten the load. You know that this person will fall to their death, but you and the other two will be able to resist until help arrives.
moral_expl: Intentionally sacrificing a helpless individual for saving others is deeply immoral, despite potential survival benefits.

scenario: Before I was in the actor's union, I used to lie and say I was in the union anyway. When you are working as an actor doing background work, it really changes how they treat you if you are a union member. So I would pretend I was in the union.
moral_expl: Intentional deception for personal gain, minor harm to integrity, yet limited overall harm.

scenario: Helped a neighbor move his piano
moral_expl: Intentional, beneficial act with minimal harm and no exploitation of vulnerable individuals.

scenario: Tripped someone in the hall
moral_expl: Intentional act causing potential physical harm with no benefits; thus it is morally unacceptable.

scenario: Person X jumped off a boat to save a drowning friend even though this put PersonX's own life at risk.
moral_expl: Selfless, intentional rescue with high benefit to a vulnerable life and minimal collateral harm.

scenario: For some time your knee has been hurting when you walk. You therefore decided to make an appointment with an orthopaedic consultant, which has been fixed for a month’s time. Several days ago, you told a friend about your problem and he was able to organize an appointment for you much sooner than the one you fixed.You go to the appointment which your friend organized, without bothering to cancel the one you booked. You know that many patients’ problems are serious.
moral_expl: Intentional double-booking misuses scarce healthcare slots, denying potential care for others despite personal benefit.

scenario: I remember getting married when I was 17 years old. I got married just so I could get away from my mother. I certainly felt relieved to separate myself from her.
moral_expl: A vulnerable minor intentionally escaped an oppressive situation, gaining personal relief with limited harm.

scenario: Person X put on a suit and wore their lucky socks in preparation for a job interview.
moral_expl: No harm done; intended self-improvement with minimal vulnerability and clear positive personal impact.

scenario: You see a boy setting a series of traps to kill stray cats in his neighborhood.
moral_expl: The boy deliberately harms vulnerable stray cats, causing significant suffering without any clear beneficial outcome.

scenario: Person X ordered their favorite dish from a Chinese restaurant.
moral_expl: Intentional, harmless choice by a non-vulnerable individual; morally neutral overall.

scenario: Volunteered his time as a big brother to a fatherless child
moral_expl: Volunteered support to a vulnerable child; intentional, harmless, highly beneficial, and morally praiseworthy.

scenario: Worked on a campaign to release prisoners of war
moral_expl: Intentionally aiding vulnerable prisoners with significant benefits and minimal harm in a humanitarian context.

scenario: You see a girl ignoring her father's orders by taking the car after her curfew.
moral_expl: The minor intentionally disobeys, showing vulnerability and risk; potential harm outweighs any benefits.

scenario: Sells drugs to high school sudents
moral_expl: Targets highly vulnerable minors; intentional, causing significant harm with no beneficial effects.

scenario: When my first husband and I split up, I was seventeen years old with a six-month-old baby and working in a grocery store. Sometimes I would just take things I needed. I remember taking cans of tuna, milk and loaves of bread.
moral_expl: A vulnerable teen mother acted out of survival necessity, minimal harm, and limited intent toward malice.

scenario: Person X was unable to fix the dripping faucet so they had to call the plumber.
moral_expl: Person X responsibly sought help; minimal harm and no vulnerable exploitation occurred.

scenario: My friends and I were going to the movies and I was taking this guy with me in my car. He was being a jerk about my driving so I decided to go really fast and drive recklessly. It got to the point where he told me to stop the car so he could get out.
moral_expl: Intentional reckless driving endangered lives and neglected others' safety for personal vindication.

scenario: You see a mother forcing her daughter to enroll as a pre-med student in college.
moral_expl: Coercion curtails the vulnerable daughter's autonomy and growth, intentionally imposing choices that likely cause psychological and personal harm.

scenario: Smiled and greeted people while walking down the street
moral_expl: Intentional, harmless, positive interaction with people who are not vulnerable, hence highly moral.

scenario: Was arrested on suspicion of child beating
moral_expl: Child, highly vulnerable; act intentional with severe harm and no benefit, making it extremely immoral.

scenario: You see a woman using a fork to eat a bowl of vanilla ice cream and marshmallows.
moral_expl: Voluntary, non-harmful action with no vulnerability; a harmless act deserving full moral acceptance.

scenario: You are on board a ship which is sinking. You are heading for the deck where the lifeboats are together with seven other people. You and another person have just crossed through a watertight door when it starts to close quickly. The other six people are behind the door, too far away to be able to pass through it in time.You knock the person next to you unconscious and use his body to block the door open. You know that this person will be crushed to death, but the other six will have time to save themselves.
moral_expl: Saving six lives by deliberately condemning one person’s death, with no consent and high vulnerability, is highly immoral.

scenario: Gave his seat to someone on the crowded bus
moral_expl: Intentionally aiding a vulnerable person with minimal harm and significant help makes this action highly moral.

scenario: Told the owner of a store that she gave him too much change
moral_expl: Honest correction that avoids unjust gain; action is intentional, low risk, and produces more help than harm.

scenario: I used to work for the federal government. I used a calculator from work at home for some audits. When I left government services, I kept the calculator.
moral_expl: Intentional use and retention of government property is unethical, a misappropriation of work resources despite causing minimal direct harm.

scenario: You see a judge taking on a criminal case although he is friends with the defendant.
moral_expl: Judge's friendship jeopardizes impartiality and trust, causing moderate bias and harm despite intentions to serve justice.

scenario: Translated menu items for a foreigner in a restaurant
moral_expl: Translating menu items benefits a vulnerable individual, is intentional and clearly helpful with no harm caused.

scenario: Person X bribed a landlord to be the first to get their apartment repainted.
moral_expl: Intentional bribery undermines fairness, causing corruption and preferential treatment with negligible benefit and ethical harm.

scenario: You are in charge of the division of a factory where wood panels are made. Because of a malfunction in one of the main machines, three workers have been caught up in a roller which is pulling them quickly into a very large press. The emergency system which blocks the machine has not activated and time is running out.You push the worker next to you into the cogs of the machine which operates the press to block the system. You know that this worker will die crushed, but the other three workers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Intentionally sacrificing an innocent, highly vulnerable worker to save others is a clear, deliberate harm.

scenario: You have taken your DVD player to be repaired at the shopping centre where you bought it. The technician told you it would cost €100 to repair. Some days later, passing by an electronics shop, you see a new and slightly better DVD reader in the window on sale for €120.You think for a bit and decide to buy the new DVD player, knowing well that the other one has already been repaired. You did not leave any contact details, and you will not go there again.
moral_expl: Intentional fee avoidance wastes technician effort, but harm is minimal and no vulnerable party is exploited.

scenario: Allowed an evicted friend to stay with him temporarily
moral_expl: Intentionally offered shelter to a vulnerable person with minimal harm and significant help.

scenario: One summer I had a job on the beach picking up trash. I found a wallet on the beach and it had $60 in it. So I picked it up and turned it in to my supervisor.
moral_expl: The act was intentional, honest, and beneficial with no harm, showing exemplary behavior.

scenario: You see a man turn his back and walk away while his boss questions his work.
moral_expl: Action appears intentional but causes minimal harm with unclear help; somewhat irresponsible but not extremely immoral.

scenario: I was on vacation and rented a car. I paid for the parking fee and the guy gave me the wrong change back. I knew he gave me too much but I kept it anyway.
moral_expl: Intentional act with minimal harm yet clearly dishonest, making it morally objectionable.

scenario: Person X closed the elevator door before an elderly neighbor could get in.
moral_expl: Elderly neighbor is vulnerable; intentional exclusion causes inconvenience and potential harm, making it morally wrong.

scenario: I lent my friend a large amount of money to get his car out of impound. He said he would pay me back, but then his wife showed up after several years. She took him for all his money, even the money he set aside for me, and he was embarrassed to tell me.
moral_expl: Wife exploited friend’s vulnerability through intentional deception, causing significant harm with minimal help provided.

scenario: Person X told a coworker that PersonX's brother was HIV-positive, a secret that their brother had told them in confidence.
moral_expl: Intentional breach of confidential info, exposing a vulnerable person to harm with no clear beneficial outcome.

scenario: I took my sister's ring that was given to her by our father's aunt. I had never met our fatherÍs aunt but my sister spent some time with her. I took the ring because I didn't think that my sister deserved it.
moral_expl: Intentional theft causing familial harm and betrayal with no help, making it significantly immoral.

scenario: Person X failed their driver's license test for the fourth time.
moral_expl: The event shows no intentional harm or vulnerability, resulting in a neutral moral outcome.

scenario: Stole Halloween candy
moral_expl: Intentional theft causing loss and undermining trust; little help while exploiting others' vulnerability.

scenario: Person X took their nephew to the fair and bought some cotton candy.
moral_expl: Nephew, although vulnerable, benefited from a safe, enjoyable fair outing with no harm involved.

scenario: One night I was going out late and I didn't want my son to know about it. I was a single mom and at that point in time he always seemed to want to act like the parent. So I snuck out of the house to go out.
moral_expl: Intentional self-care with minor deception, minimal harm, and protection of personal space outweigh potential trust issues.

scenario: Person X helped paint their neighbor's house even though it was PersonX's birthday.
moral_expl: Altruistic action sacrificing personal joy for neighbor’s benefit; no harm occurred and intention was positive.

scenario: A few years ago I was living alone. I had an enormous stereo system in my apartment and I used to turn it up very loud. One night I was drunk and turned it up to its maximum volume.
moral_expl: Drunken, unintentional behavior with minimal harm and vulnerability, primarily affecting only noise levels.

scenario: Someone makes a post on Facebook about how people with disabilities are a drain on society
moral_expl: Intentional hate speech against vulnerable disabled individuals causing significant harm with no benefit.

scenario: Shoved a man who was passing out leaflets
moral_expl: Intentional physical aggression causing harm without benefit; victim not severely vulnerable but not justified.

scenario: Circulated a petition in support of equal pay for women

scenario: I email women through the personal ads quite often. I lie about many things such as awards I haven’t actually won, or places I’ve been. I’m pretty sure everyone lies about things in those ads to make themselves more attractive to the people that they are interested in.

scenario: Someone posts pornographic self-photography online
moral_expl: Consent given, self-expression intentional, minimal harm, no undue vulnerability involved.

scenario: You see a man putting ketchup all over his chicken Caesar salad while at lunch.
moral_expl: Intentional, harmless expression of personal taste with no vulnerable party affected.

scenario: Person X promised to look after their elderly mother's dog but then secretly sold it as soon as their mother moved into the nursing home.
moral_expl: Exploited an elderly person's trust intentionally, causing significant harm with no benefit provided.

scenario: You are in charge of a team of specialised technicians who are carrying out maintenance work on a very large lift. Six technicians are working in the lift well. You and another technician are on the top floor in the machine-room. Suddenly, the winch breaks and the lift cabin starts to plummet downwards.You push the technician next to you into the cogs of the mechanism so that his body will cause the lift to stop. You know that he will die, but the six technicians will be saved.
moral_expl: The technician was highly vulnerable; his death was intentionally caused as a means to save others, violating intrinsic rights.

scenario: You see a man snickering as he passes by a cancer patient with a bald head.
moral_expl: The patient was vulnerable; the act was intentional, causing emotional harm with no benefit.

scenario: You see a man secretly watching sports on his cell phone during a pastor's sermon.
moral_expl: Intentional disrespect in a sacred context causing minor harm with no benefit makes it somewhat morally objectionable.

scenario: Person X offered to let their evicted sister and brother-in-law stay with them for free and sleep in PersonX's room while PersonX slept on the couch.
moral_expl: Sheltering vulnerable relatives is an intentional, self-sacrificial act with minimal harm and significant benefit.

scenario: Person X started a vicious rumor about their ex-partner, saying that they had neglected their children when they were still married.
moral_expl: Intentional malicious rumor exploiting a vulnerable target with significant harm and no benefit.

scenario: My friend was always putting down all the men I dated because she was jealous. I confronted her about it and said some really mean comments to her about how no man wants her. I hurt her feelings a lot.
moral_expl: Intentional harm causing significant emotional damage with no benefit; friend’s vulnerability was exploited.

scenario: Person X risked their life rescuing an animal that was trapped inside a burning house.
moral_expl: The animal was extremely vulnerable; intentional rescue causing minimal harm with significant benefit.

scenario: You see a man telling his fiance that she has to switch to his political party.
moral_expl: Coercive imposition undermines autonomy, causing relational harm with minimal benefit, making this morally questionable.

scenario: You are an Air Force commander and you are in the control tower with another two officers to check some documents. During an exercise that is taking place in the surrounding air space, a plane has engine problems and is about to crash into the control tower. The pilot communicates by radio that he is about to eject from the plane.You give orders that the plane must be immediately shot down. You know that the pilot will not have time to eject and will die, but you and the other two officers will escape injury.
moral_expl: Pilot was vulnerable; ordering the shoot-down intentionally killed him to prevent harm, saving three lives despite ethical costs.

scenario: I cheated on my girlfriend even though I knew that she would find out and be upset. I cheated on her for a very long time and really hurt her. She found out and we broke up.
moral_expl: Intentionally betrayed trust, exploiting vulnerability with prolonged, significant emotional harm.

scenario: Person X forgot to put the alarm on when they were the last one to leave the office.
moral_expl: Unintentional oversight causing minimal harm with low vulnerability impact, thus morally neutral overall.

scenario: Throws a grenade into a bunker to flush out enemies.
moral_expl: Intentional combat action, aimed at enemy combatants; high harm but strategic, justified in a wartime context.

scenario: Offered to show a new student around campus after class
moral_expl: The action was intentional, low-risk, and highly beneficial to a vulnerable newcomer.

scenario: My husband and I split up and soon after he was arrested for drug use. While he was in jail I used some of his credit cards that I still had from our marriage. I did it so that he would be so upset with me that he would stop trying to contact me.
moral_expl: Deliberate misuse causing financial and emotional harm with no sufficient benefit, intentionally targeting his vulnerability while he's least able to respond.

scenario: You see an intern disobeying an order to dress professionally and comb his hair.
moral_expl: Intentional minor rule-breaking with limited harm and negligible benefit, violating professionalism norms.

scenario: You are a specialist in underwater welding. You are working with your team of three other people in a small dam at a depth of 30m under water. Because of a problem with the flow regulator, about 100 cubic metres too much water has flowed into the dam. The pressure is too great and you are unable to surface.You activate the opening of the sluice gates. The water will flow into the valley where there is an observatory. You know that a researcher works there, and he/she will be swept away in the overflow water, but you and the other three will be saved.
moral_expl: Intentionally sacrificing a defenseless researcher for self-preservation is a grave and immoral action.

scenario: Made an obscene gesture at an old lady
moral_expl: The intentional, disrespectful gesture toward a vulnerable individual causes harm, offering no benefit.

scenario: Person X taught their nephew how to drive at the local shopping center car park, after the shops had shut.
moral_expl: Safe learning environment; intentional act with minimal harm and clear benefit to the learner.

scenario: My sister is five years older than I am. When we were little she would always tease me about things. So one day when she was teasing me about something, I threw a pencil at her.
moral_expl: Intentional aggression causing minimal harm, with no significant benefit, though context may lessen severity.

scenario: Person X learned how to play the piano when they were a child.
moral_expl: Child learning piano is intentional, non-harmful, and beneficial despite inherent vulnerability.

scenario: One night I was having sex with my boyfriend. He said that he had a condom on but at the end I found out that he didn't. I became pregnant and since I just had had a baby recently, I decided to have an abortion.
moral_expl: Boyfriend’s deception exploited postpartum vulnerability, inflicting harm that outweighs her autonomous abortion choice.

scenario: You see a Hollywood star agreeing with a foreign dictator's denunciation of the US.
moral_expl: A public figure intentionally endorses a dictator’s harmful view, undermining democratic values and causing political divisiveness.

scenario: Person X told the children to be quiet in the library.
moral_expl: Intentional, low harm act promoting respect; children’s slight vulnerability offset by creating a beneficial, quiet space.

scenario: Spent time one summer teaching his daughter to play baseball

scenario: Person X started a false rumor that their office colleague Mary used to be a sex worker.
